title: JDK, JVM and Java language deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for JDK, JVM and Java language; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: JDK, JVM and Java language: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? The JDK, Java language, bytecode, and JVM are four names for the same component and always have the same version behavior.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Treat language rules, JDK tools, class-file bytecode, and the JVM as separate layers with explicit compatibility; javac produces class-file bytecode for a declared release.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Treat language rules, JDK tools, class-file bytecode, and the JVM as separate layers with explicit compatibility; javac produces class-file bytecode for a declared release.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Treat language rules, JDK tools, class-file bytecode, and the JVM as separate layers with explicit compatibility; javac produces class-file bytecode for a declared release.
The JDK, Java language, bytecode, and JVM are four names for the same component and always have the same version behavior.

answer.explanation: Treat language rules, JDK tools, class-file bytecode, and the JVM as separate layers with explicit compatibility; javac produces class-file bytecode for a declared release.
